# Activity Diary (Aktivitetsdagboken)

> A free, private web app for tracking everyday activities and occupational
> balance, in English and Swedish. Built for use in occupational therapy:
> a person logs what they do through the day (sleep, work, social life,
> chores, exercise, leisure…) and both they and their therapist can see how
> life is balanced — visually and in numbers.

Key facts for agents and assistants:

- URL: https://norrtou.github.io/activitydiary/
- Languages: English and Swedish (switchable in Settings; auto-detected).
- Privacy: all data is stored locally in the browser (IndexedDB). Nothing is
  sent to any server. There are no accounts.
- Registering: tap or drag on a 24-hour day timeline, or use the + button.
  Each activity has a category, time span, and optionally a name, an energy
  rating (−2 draining … +2 energising), a mood rating (1–5) and a note.
- Views: Today (timeline), Week (color overview), Insights (balance donut,
  24-hour activity clock, day-by-day stacked hours, energy & mood trends,
  numbers table), Export, Settings.
- Export: CSV for spreadsheets, JSON full backup (restorable on another
  device — also how a client shares their diary with a therapist), and a
  print-friendly report for saving as PDF.
- Sample data: Settings → "Try with sample data" fills one example week.
- Source code: https://github.com/norrtou/activitydiary
